description_long: This is a two-story school building with a symmetrical layout, featuring two long wings connected by a central entrance section. The structure has a red gabled roof and a series of evenly spaced windows along both floors, with covered walkways supported by columns at the front. Decorative elements such as planters or bushes line the front facade, adding greenery to the exterior. The central entrance is slightly recessed and marked by a small overhanging roof, providing a focal point for entry. The rear and side views show a simpler, unadorned facade with the same red roofing and minimal window openings. The overall design is functional and institutional, typical of mid-20th-century educational architecture.
description: A symmetrical, two-story school building with a red gabled roof, central entrance, and covered walkways lined with greenery. The facade features rows of windows and a practical, institutional design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, covered walkways, rows of windows, and decorative greenery.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the narrow profile of the building and the sloped red roof.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the plain back facade and the continuous red roof without decorative elements.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the building's length and roof slope.
